domain: YouTube creator growth and strategy, the developer API (Data, Analytics, Reporting, Live Streaming), and paid advertising (Google Ads)
refresh_cadence: monthly for YouTube Help Center and Studio analytics pages; on-changelog for recommendation and monetization policy; quarterly for practitioner benchmarks
provenance: Six official-source-biased deep-research passes on 2026-06-23 (WebSearch + WebFetch). Full citation pack in wiki/sources/research-pack-2026-06-23.md; raw capture in .raw/research/youtube-deep-research-2026-06-23.md.

researched_requires: at least two official or primary sources for this fast-moving domain
retrieved and refresh_due dates, source_type, confidence, and claim coverage on every entry

sources.title: How YouTube recommendations work
claims: The recommendation system processes over 80 billion signals.
Eight named personalization signals: watch history, search history, subscriptions, likes, dislikes, not interested, don't recommend channel, and satisfaction surveys.
Recommendations surface on the homepage, Up Next, the Shorts feed, destination pages, and channel pages.

sources.title: YouTube's Recommendation System
claims: The system has two objectives: help viewers find content they want and maximize long-term satisfaction.
It operates as automated word-of-mouth, learning that viewers of one video also watch another.

sources.title: How YouTube search works
claims: Search ranks on three pillars: relevance, engagement, and quality.
Engagement for search is the watch time of a video for a particular query.
YouTube does not accept payment for better organic search placement.

sources.title: Search and discovery tips
claims: The system uses average view duration and average percentage viewed as retention signals.
Core guidance: focus on what your audience likes and the recommendations will follow.

sources.title: Algorithm-Based Recommendations on YouTube
claims: The homepage and the Up Next panel are the two main recommendation surfaces.
Authoritative content is elevated for news, finance, and health topics.

sources.title: On YouTube's recommendation system
claims: Watch time was introduced as a signal in 2012.
YouTube measures valued watchtime using 1-5 star surveys where only 4-5 star ratings count.
The goal is to keep recommended borderline content below 0.5% of overall views.

sources.title: How YouTube's Recommendation System Works In 2025
claims: YouTube's Todd Beaupre describes recommendation as pulling content toward each viewer, not pushing it out.
The system weights watch time by satisfaction signals like surveys, returns, and session continuation.

sources.title: A Change to How We Count Views on Shorts
claims: From 2025-03-31 a Shorts view counts every time a Short starts to play or replay, with no minimum watch time.
The previous sustained-watch metric is renamed engaged views and still governs YPP eligibility and Shorts revenue sharing.

sources.title: 9 new features we announced at Made on YouTube 2024
claims: Hype was introduced to help small-to-medium creators get discovered.
The Studio Inspiration Tab gives AI-generated ideas, titles, thumbnails, and outlines.

sources.title: Hype goes global
claims: Hype expanded to 39 countries in August 2025.
Eligibility is creators with under 500,000 subscribers; viewers hype up to three videos per week.
Smaller channels receive proportionally larger boosts.

sources.title: Leaderboard for hyped videos on YouTube
claims: The leaderboard shows up to 50 top hyped videos, filterable by topic.
The fewer the subscribers, the bigger the bonus; the page states the directional rule but not specific point values.

sources.title: Measure key moments for audience retention
claims: Four key moment types: Intro, Top moments, Spikes, and Dips.
The Intro metric measures the percentage of viewers still watching after the first 30 seconds.
Key moments require a video at least 60 seconds long with at least 100 views.

sources.title: Content tab analytics tips
claims: A typical intro is about 30 seconds; strong videos keep 50% or more of the audience past the 30-second mark.
The performance funnel is Appeal (CTR), Engagement (Views), and Satisfaction (average view duration).

sources.title: Understand audience engagement
claims: Average view duration is the average minutes watched among those who stayed to watch.
For Shorts, average view duration is calculated from engaged views.

sources.title: New YouTube Shorts Metric: Viewed vs Swiped Away
claims: The Viewed vs swiped away metric reports the percentage of times viewers watched a Short versus swiped past it in the feed.

sources.title: Understanding YouTube Average View Duration
claims: AVD equals total watch time divided by views; APV equals AVD divided by video length times 100.
An APV of 50-60% is good, 60-70% very good, and 70%+ excellent.

sources.title: Beyond Views: The 2025 State of YouTube Audience Retention
claims: Across 10,000+ videos average retention is 23.7%.
55%+ of viewers are lost within the first 60 seconds, and only 16.8% of videos exceed 50% retention.

sources.title: Impressions and click-through-rate FAQs
claims: Half of all channels and videos have an impressions CTR between 2% and 10%.
Impressions CTR measures how often viewers watched a video after seeing a registered impression.

sources.title: Decoding CTR and impressions in your Analytics
claims: A CTR decline from 9% to 3.5% across 90,000 added impressions is a sign of success.
Search yields fewer impressions but higher CTR; the homepage yields high impressions but lower CTR.

sources.title: A/B test titles and thumbnails (Test and compare)
claims: Creators test up to 3 titles, thumbnails, or combinations.
The winner is the option with the highest watch-time share, not the highest CTR.
Outcomes are Winner, Performed the same, or Inconclusive, and YouTube may keep a small control group.

sources.title: Thumbnail and title tips
claims: 90% of the best-performing videos have custom thumbnails.
Front-load important words in titles and use the rule of thirds for thumbnails.

sources.title: Spam, deceptive practices and scams policies
claims: Maliciously misleading titles, thumbnails, or descriptions are prohibited.
Violations risk removal, strikes, and monetization suspension.

sources.title: New in YouTube Studio (Made On YouTube 2025)
claims: Thumbnail A/B testing has been used over 15 million times since it launched in 2023.
Title testing was added so creators can test up to three titles and thumbnails per video.

sources.title: YouTube Title A/B Testing Rolls Out Globally To Creators
claims: Title A/B testing expanded to all eligible creators around 2025-12-04.
Variations are shown for up to two weeks, then YouTube applies the highest-watch-time option.

sources.title: YouTube Shorts monetization policies
claims: Creators keep 45% of revenue allocated to them from the Shorts Creator Pool.
Music reduces a Short's pool contribution: no music sends 100% to the pool, one track 50%, two tracks roughly one-third.

sources.title: YouTube partner earnings overview
claims: Watch Page ads pay creators 55% of net revenue; Shorts Feed ads pay 45%.
Fan funding (memberships, Super Chat, Super Stickers, Super Thanks) pays creators 70%.

sources.title: YouTube Partner Program overview and eligibility
claims: Full monetization requires 1,000 subscribers plus 4,000 valid public watch hours in 12 months or 10 million valid public Shorts views in 90 days.
Valid public watch hours exclude private, unlisted, deleted videos, ad campaigns, and Shorts.

sources.title: Overview of the expanded YouTube Partner Program
claims: The lower fan-funding tier requires 500 subscribers, 3 valid public uploads in 90 days, and 3,000 watch hours in 12 months or 3 million Shorts views in 90 days.
This tier unlocks memberships, Super Chat, Super Thanks, gifts, and Shopping before full ad monetization.

sources.title: Understand three-minute YouTube Shorts
claims: Standard channels can upload Shorts up to 3 minutes for uploads on or after 2024-10-15.
Most songs are usable for up to 90 seconds in a 3-minute Short.

sources.title: YouTube is changing how YouTube Shorts views are counted
claims: Effective 2025-03-31, a Shorts view counts each time the Short starts to play or replay.
The old metric is renamed engaged views and still governs earnings and YPP eligibility.

sources.title: YouTube channel monetization policies
claims: On 2025-07-15 repetitious content was renamed inauthentic content, targeting mass-produced or templated material.
Reused content can still monetize with significant original commentary or substantive modification.

sources.title: Advertiser-friendly content guidelines
claims: Three monetization icons: green (full ad revenue), yellow (limited or no ads), red (no ad revenue).
The red icon commonly indicates an active copyright claim; the icon definitions live in the monetization icon guide (9208564) and the limited-or-no-ads page (9269824).

sources.title: Manage mid-roll ad breaks in long videos
claims: Videos must be 8 minutes or longer to enable mid-roll ads.
Ad slots at natural breakpoints are more likely to serve; disruptive placements are flagged.

sources.title: Mid-roll ads updates explained
claims: Combining automatic and manual mid-roll slots showed an average 5% revenue increase.
The opt-out deadline for adding automatic slots to existing manual-only videos was 2025-05-12.

sources.title: YouTube Shopping affiliate program overview and eligibility
claims: The Shopping affiliate program is open to YPP creators with at least 500 subscribers.
Each brand sets its own commission rate and attribution window.

sources.title: YouTube RPM Explained
claims: RPM equals total revenue divided by total views times 1,000 across all revenue sources after YouTube's cut.
RPM by niche: finance $4-$12, business $4-$9, entertainment $1-$2, Shorts $0.01-$0.07.

sources.title: Understand your YouTube content performance
claims: The official funnel is impressions, click-through rate, views, and watch time.
Subscribers driven is the funnel endpoint, broken out by content type.

sources.title: Understand your YouTube video reach
claims: Traffic-source types include Browse features, Suggested videos, YouTube search, Channel pages, External, Notifications, Playlists, and Direct or unknown.

sources.title: Check your YouTube impressions and watch time
claims: A registered impression counts only when a thumbnail is shown for more than 1 second and at least 50% visible.
Impressions are not counted for external or embedded players, notifications, or background-tab playback.

sources.title: Understand your YouTube audience
claims: Returning viewers already watched the channel and came back; new viewers watched for the first time.
Monthly audience is a rolling 28-day estimate; the report shows when viewers are on YouTube.

sources.title: Upload schedule tips
claims: A consistent, sustainable release schedule builds and fulfills audience expectations.
YouTube advises scheduling tools and batch filming to stay consistent.

sources.title: Good to know about recommendations
claims: There is no universal ideal length for YouTube videos; avoid filler and artificial stretching.
An individual video's underperformance does not penalize a channel overall.

sources.title: How Often to Post on YouTube (5 Million Channels)
claims: Across 5 million channels, posting 12+ videos per month generates 53% more views and 66% more subscribers than posting 1-3 times per month.

sources.title: A new YouTube Communities experience for fans, by fans
claims: Communities is a fan-driven space where fans post and reply; to start, only subscribers can post.

sources.title: YouTube Data API Overview
claims: Default quota is 10,000 units/day plus separate 100 search.list and 100 videos.insert calls/day.
API keys read public data; insert, update, and delete always require OAuth 2.0.

sources.title: Quota Calculator
claims: List reads cost 1 unit; writes cost 50 units; captions.insert 400 units and captions.update 450 units.

sources.title: Quota and Compliance Audits
claims: Exceeding the default quota requires passing a compliance audit against the API Terms of Service.

sources.title: Implementing OAuth 2.0 Authorization
claims: Scopes (youtube.readonly, youtube, youtube.force-ssl, youtube.upload, youtubepartner) define what an app can do as the user.

sources.title: YouTube Data API Reference
claims: Resources include search, videos, channels, playlists, playlistItems, comments, captions, subscriptions, thumbnails, watermarks.

sources.title: Search: list
claims: search.list returns IDs and snippets only, maxResults 0-50, costs 1 unit but is capped at 100 calls/day.

sources.title: Videos: insert
claims: videos.insert requires OAuth and supports resumable upload up to 256 GB.

sources.title: Analytics and Reporting APIs Introduction
claims: Analytics API serves real-time targeted queries; Reporting API serves bulk predefined daily reports.

sources.title: Get Bulk Data Reports
claims: jobs.create with a reportTypeId produces daily CSVs available within 48 hours; about 30 days of backfill.

sources.title: YouTube Live Streaming API Overview
claims: liveBroadcasts, liveStreams, and cuepoint; bind links them and transition advances ready, testing, live; OAuth for writes.

sources.title: YouTube API Services Developer Policies
claims: Authorized API data must be deleted or refreshed within 30 days; user deletions within 7 days; no scraping or fake engagement.

sources.title: YouTube Data API Revision History
claims: 2026-06-01 introduced granular quota buckets; 2025-12-04 cut upload cost to about 100 units.

sources.title: About video ad formats
claims: Skippable in-stream skips after 5s; bumper 6s; non-skippable up to 60s; in-feed in search and related; Masthead reservation-only.

sources.title: About cost-per-view (CPV) bidding
claims: An in-stream view counts at 30s or full ad or interaction; in-feed at click or 10s autoplay; Shorts at 10s, full, or CTA.

sources.title: Video Action Campaigns upgraded to Demand Gen
claims: New VACs blocked from April 2025; auto-upgrades to Demand Gen from July 2025; final upgrades by April 2026.

sources.title: Upgrading Video Action Campaigns to Demand Gen
claims: Video plus image assets in Demand Gen drove 20% more conversions at the same CPA; Demand Gen reaches 3 billion monthly users.

sources.title: About Demand Gen campaigns
claims: Demand Gen serves across YouTube, Shorts, Discover, Gmail, Maps, and Display; bids Maximize Conversions, Target CPA, Target ROAS, Maximize Clicks.

sources.title: Video reach campaigns
claims: Three subtypes: efficient reach, non-skippable reach, and target frequency.

sources.title: About Target frequency
claims: Multi-format frequency targets 2-7 weekly or 4-12 monthly; an average target, not a cap.

sources.title: About Video views
claims: Video views campaigns use multi-format ads on Target CPV and can deliver up to 40% more views for the budget.

sources.title: Non-skippable in-stream ads
claims: Bumper up to 6s, standard 7-15s, and a 30s connected-TV-only horizontal format; Target CPM.

sources.title: About YouTube ads and view metrics
claims: Distinguishes TrueView views from view-through conversions; earned actions tracked within 7 days; reports Unique Reach and frequency.

sources.title: About audience segments
claims: Affinity, in-market, life events, detailed demographics, custom, and your-data segments; no sensitive-data targeting.

sources.title: Restricted targeting in Personalized advertising
claims: Sensitive categories are restricted; the DSA bans targeting on sensitive data in the EU and EEA.

sources.title: Brandcast 2025
claims: Announced an immersive CTV Masthead and Shoppable CTV for the living-room screen.

sources.title: Google launches non-skippable Video Reach for CTV
claims: VRC non-skip launched globally on 2026-03-03 supporting 6s, 15s, and 30s CTV ads.

sources.title: More Shoppable CTV Ads On YouTube Now
claims: Shoppable CTV rolled out 2026-01-23; QR codes lifted conversions over 100%; pulls from Merchant Center feeds.

sources.title: Add end screens to videos
claims: Video must be 25+ seconds; end screens use the last 5-20 seconds; up to four elements on 16:9; not on made-for-kids.

sources.title: Add info cards to videos
claims: Up to five cards per video in Video, Playlist, Channel, and Link types; blocked on made-for-kids.

sources.title: Series playlists
claims: An official series cross-features its videos; a video can appear in only one series playlist.

sources.title: Video Chapters
claims: First timestamp must be 00:00, at least three timestamps, minimum 10-second chapters.

sources.title: Add tags to your YouTube videos
claims: Tags play a minimal role in discovery and are mainly useful for commonly misspelled terms.

sources.title: Use automatic dubbing
claims: Auto dubbing dubs into English from 29 languages and out of English into 20; skips 120+ minute or Content-ID-claimed videos.

sources.title: Unlocking a global audience with auto dubbing
claims: Auto dubbing available in 27 languages; over 6 million daily viewers watched dubbed content in December.

sources.title: Customize your YouTube channel layout
claims: Channel trailer for non-subscribers and spotlight for returning subscribers; up to 12 custom sections.

sources.title: Explore Inspiration tab
claims: Generates AI ideas, titles, thumbnails, and outlines; prompts deleted after 45 days; desktop and English only.

sources.title: Understand copyright strikes
claims: Three copyright strikes in 90 days can terminate a channel; a strike expires 90 days after Copyright School; a Content ID claim is not a strike.

sources.title: Learn about Content ID claims
claims: A Content ID claim is auto-generated, is not a strike, and usually does not impact the channel.

sources.title: Community Guidelines strike basics
claims: First strike one-week freeze, second two weeks, three strikes in 90 days can terminate; deleting content does not remove a strike.

sources.title: Set your channel or video's audience
claims: Made-for-kids is a COPPA requirement that disables comments, cards, end screens, personalized ads, memberships, and more.

sources.title: Collaboration tips
claims: A collaboration can be recommended to both audiences; choose partners by audience compatibility, not subscriber count.

sources.title: Monetize your live stream
claims: Live automated mid-rolls averaged over 20% uplift in in-stream ad revenue per hour; Supers pay creators 70%.

sources.title: Highest-Paying YouTube Niches
claims: Finance, insurance, and legal command the highest CPMs (about $15-$50); creator RPM runs 30-50% below CPM.

sources.title: Subscribe to YouTube channels
claims: Subscribing surfaces a channel's new uploads in the viewer's Subscriptions feed.
A viewer can have up to 75 new subscriptions per day.

sources.title: Manage YouTube notifications
claims: The notification bell offers All, Personalized, and None settings.
Made-for-kids channels force the bell to no notifications.

sources.title: Fix subscriber notification problems
claims: Not every upload triggers a notification; delivery is timed by viewer signals.
A channel can send at most 3 notifications per subscriber per 24 hours.
Personalized subscribers still see every upload in the Subscriptions feed even without a push.

sources.title: Check subscriber notifications
claims: The all-notifications reach metric counts only subscribers on All with device notifications enabled.
Notifications are one of many traffic sources for views.

sources.title: Change your subscription privacy settings
claims: Subscriptions are private by default.
A channel's subscriber list only shows subscribers who made their subscriptions public.

sources.title: YouTube Creator Awards
claims: Creator Awards thresholds: Silver 100k, Gold 1M, Diamond 10M, Red Diamond 100M subscribers.
Eligibility requires a count free of artificially inflated subscribers.

sources.title: Get started with channel memberships
claims: Channel memberships let viewers pay a recurring monthly fee for perks.
Viewers must be 18+ to join a membership.

sources.title: Analyze and manage your channel memberships program
claims: Creators get 70% of membership revenue after applicable taxes and fees.
YouTube currently covers transaction and credit-card costs.

sources.title: Pricing for channel memberships (United States)
claims: US membership price points range from $0.99 to $499.99 per month.
A pricing-options update began rolling out from May 2025.

sources.title: Turn on and manage Super Thanks
claims: Super Thanks is a one-time purchase on watch pages for long-form videos and Shorts.
Creators get 70% of Super Thanks revenue after taxes and fees.

sources.title: Super Thanks eligibility, availability, and policies
claims: Super Thanks is available to eligible creators in 150+ countries.
Not available on live streams, age-restricted, made-for-kids, or private videos.

sources.title: Super Chat & Super Stickers eligibility, availability, and policies
claims: Super Chat and Super Stickers are purchases during live streams and Premieres.
Available to eligible creators in 140+ countries.

sources.title: Buy a Super Chat or Super Sticker
claims: Viewer spend is capped at $500/day and $2,000/week across all Supers and gifts combined.
Purchases under $5 do not appear in the ticker.

sources.title: Gift channel memberships on YouTube
claims: Gifted memberships grant up to one month of perks and then expire.
Gifts are non-recurring and non-refundable and occur during live streams or Premieres.

sources.title: Get started with Shopping on YouTube
claims: YouTube Shopping splits into tagging your own products and the affiliate program.
Own-store Shopping requires YPP and the subscriber threshold.

sources.title: Connect, manage, & disconnect your store on YouTube
claims: Supported store platforms include Shopify, Wix, Fourthwall, Spreadshop, Spring, and SHOPLINE.
Connected products are reviewed for policy and Merchant Center compliance.

sources.title: Expanding YouTube Shopping to creators with 500+ subscribers
claims: As of March 2026 the affiliate program is open to YPP creators with at least 500 subscribers.
Affiliate commissions are paid via AdSense 60-120 days after purchase.

sources.title: Distribute podcasts on YouTube
claims: A YouTube podcast is a playlist whose episodes are videos.
Podcasts are included in YouTube Music with background play without Premium.

sources.title: Deliver podcasts using an RSS feed
claims: RSS ingestion auto-creates a video per episode from the show art.
RSS will not refresh show details or notify subscribers for back-catalog episodes.

sources.title: Take a course on YouTube
claims: Ad-supported courses became globally available on 2025-10-15.
Paid one-time-payment courses remain a limited test; most revenue goes to the creator.

sources.title: Create a course in YouTube Studio
claims: Creating a course requires advanced features access and at least 3 original teaching videos.
Courses cannot reuse videos from other channels or be age-restricted.

sources.title: The next chapter for Learning on YouTube
claims: Courses can be free and ad-supported or paid and ad-free with background play.

sources.title: Playables on YouTube
claims: Playables are games inside YouTube requiring no Premium subscription.
Availability is region-gated to seven territories including the US, UK, and Canada.

sources.title: Add or remove access to your channel with channel permissions
claims: Channel permissions roles include Owner, Manager, Editor, Editor (Limited), Subtitle Editor, Viewer, and Viewer (Limited).
Channel permission invites expire after 30 days.

sources.title: Manage your Brand Account
claims: A Brand Account can have multiple owners and managers and must have one primary owner.
You must be an owner for 7 days before deleting or restoring the account.

sources.title: Change who manages your Brand Account
claims: Brand Account roles are primary owner, owner, manager, and communications manager.
You must be an owner for 7 days before you can remove owners or managers.

sources.title: Change channel owners & managers with a Brand Account
claims: Ownership can only be transferred when the channel is on a Brand Account.
Both parties must have held an owner or manager role for 7 days to transfer primary ownership.

sources.title: Migrate from Brand Account user access to channel permissions
claims: YouTube is migrating channel sharing from Brand Account roles to Studio channel permissions for security.

sources.title: Learn about feature access for YouTube creators
claims: Feature access has standard, intermediate, and advanced tiers.
Intermediate features require phone verification; advanced require channel history or ID/video verification.

sources.title: Learn about YouTube handles
claims: A handle is a unique channel identifier starting with @ and becomes the channel URL.
Standard handles are 3-30 characters.

sources.title: Understand your YouTube channel's URLs
claims: The handle URL format is youtube.com/@handle and is created automatically.
Legacy custom and username URLs redirect to the handle-based URL.

sources.title: Change, view, or hide your handle
claims: A handle can be changed twice within a 14-day period.
A former handle is held for 14 days before becoming available to others.

sources.title: Custom URL overview
claims: New custom URLs can no longer be created and existing ones can no longer be changed.

sources.title: Mention channels in video titles & descriptions
claims: Typing @ lets a creator mention another channel by name or handle.
Not every mention triggers a notification.

sources.title: Impersonation policy
claims: Handles or names phonetically identical or visually similar to an established entity are prohibited.

sources.title: Subscriptions resource (Data API v3)
claims: The subscriptions resource represents a user's subscription to a channel.

sources.title: Subscriptions: list
claims: subscriptions.list costs 1 unit and requires exactly one filter such as mine, channelId, or mySubscribers.
There is no mySubscriptions parameter; use mine=true.

sources.title: Members: list
claims: members.list costs 2 units and is creator-only and allowlisted.
Requires the youtube.channel-memberships.creator OAuth scope.

sources.title: MembershipsLevels: list
claims: membershipsLevels.list costs 1 unit and returns the creator's membership levels.

sources.title: Push Notifications (WebSub/PubSubHubbub)
claims: Push notifications use the WebSub hub at pubsubhubbub.appspot.com to notify on uploads and edits.
The channel upload feed topic is youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?channel_id=CHANNEL_ID.

sources.title: About content suitability
claims: Inventory modes are now named Maximum, Moderate, and Limited, replacing Expanded/Standard/Limited.
As of September 2024 YouTube ads no longer use digital content labels from brand-safety settings.

sources.title: New brand suitability controls for YouTube feeds and Discover
claims: As of November 2025 inventory types and content-theme exclusions extend to Home feed, Watch Next, and Discover.

sources.title: About the YouTube Masthead
claims: The Masthead is a reservation format at the top of the Home feed, sold as CPM or CPH.
CPH Masthead secures 100% share of voice during reserved hours.

sources.title: Create a YouTube CPM Masthead campaign
claims: CPM Masthead is bought via a Google rep, Programmatic Guaranteed, or Google Ads Reservation, usually with allowlisting.

sources.title: Tall updates coming to Shorts
claims: YouTube announced format updates to Shorts.

sources.title: YouTube Analytics API channel reports
claims: Documents YouTube Analytics API channel report dimensions and metrics.

sources.title: Registering your application
claims: Explains registering an app and obtaining API key or OAuth credentials.

sources.title: Videos: list
claims: videos.list returns snippet, contentDetails, statistics, and status parts for videos.

sources.title: Update to the Personalized ads policy
claims: A 2025-12-12 update expanded Custom Segments for Display advertisers.

sources.title: YouTube Community Guidelines enforcement
claims: Describes YouTube Community Guidelines enforcement reported in the transparency report.

sources.title: Add video chapters and key moments
claims: Explains adding chapters and key moments to videos.

sources.title: Auto dubbing and multi-language audio
claims: Documents auto dubbing and multi-language audio tracks.

sources.title: Test & Compare thumbnails
claims: Documents the native Thumbnail Test & Compare A/B feature.

sources.title: Collaborate with other creators
claims: Documents the collaboration/cross-promotion feature.

sources.title: Add subtitles and captions
claims: Explains adding subtitles and captions to videos.

sources.title: Create and manage playlists
claims: Explains creating and managing playlists and series.

sources.title: Copyright strike basics
claims: Explains how copyright strikes work and their consequences.

sources.title: Add custom video thumbnails
claims: Documents custom thumbnail requirements and specs.

sources.title: Understand YouTube Analytics (Advanced mode)
claims: Documents YouTube Studio Analytics tabs and Advanced mode data.

sources.title: Create or manage your channel's membership levels and perks
claims: Up to 6 membership levels, each with 1-5 stacked perks like badges, emoji, and members-only content.

sources.title: Manage Super Chat and Super Stickers for live chat
claims: Creators receive 70% of Supers revenue after taxes and fees.

sources.title: Learn about posts
claims: Community posts support text, polls, quizzes, images, GIFs, and video.

sources.title: Manage your channel branding
claims: Covers channel branding elements such as banner, profile picture, and watermark.

sources.title: Channel banner and profile picture tips
claims: Specifies channel banner and profile picture size and safe-area requirements.

sources.title: YouTube Self-Certification overview
claims: Self-certification lets creators rate their own videos against the advertiser-friendly guidelines.

sources.title: Premiere a new video
claims: Premieres add a countdown and live chat, then become regular videos.

sources.title: Create a live stream on mobile
claims: Mobile live streaming has subscriber-count eligibility requirements.

sources.title: Determining if your content is made for kids
claims: Explains how to set the made-for-kids audience designation under COPPA.

sources.title: How ads work on made-for-kids content
claims: Made-for-kids content serves only contextual, non-personalized ads.
